name: httpx-toolkit
title: httpx-toolkit

commands.name: httpx-toolkit

arguments.description: input file containing list of hosts to process
arguments.raw: -l, -list string         input file containing list of hosts to process

arguments.description: file containing raw request
arguments.raw: -rr, -request string     file containing raw request

arguments.description: input target host(s) to probe
arguments.raw: -u, -target string[]     input target host(s) to probe

arguments.description: mode of input file (burp)
arguments.raw: -im, -input-mode string  mode of input file (burp)

arguments.description: display response status-code
arguments.raw: -sc, -status-code                      display response status-code

arguments.description: display response content-length
arguments.raw: -cl, -content-length                   display response content-length

arguments.description: display response content-type
arguments.raw: -ct, -content-type                     display response content-type

arguments.description: display response redirect location
arguments.raw: -location                              display response redirect location

arguments.description: display mmh3 hash for '/favicon.ico' file
arguments.raw: -favicon                               display mmh3 hash for '/favicon.ico' file

arguments.description: display response body hash (supported: md5,mmh3,simhash,sha1,sha256,sha512)
arguments.raw: -hash string                           display response body hash (supported: md5,mmh3,simhash,sha1,sha256,sha512)

arguments.description: display jarm fingerprint hash
arguments.raw: -jarm                                  display jarm fingerprint hash

arguments.description: display response time
arguments.raw: -rt, -response-time                    display response time

arguments.description: display response body line count
arguments.raw: -lc, -line-count                       display response body line count

arguments.description: display response body word count
arguments.raw: -wc, -word-count                       display response body word count

arguments.description: display page title
arguments.raw: -title                                 display page title

arguments.description: display first N characters of response body (default 100)
arguments.raw: -bp, -body-preview                     display first N characters of response body (default 100)

arguments.description: display server name
arguments.raw: -server, -web-server                   display server name

arguments.description: display technology in use based on wappalyzer dataset
arguments.raw: -td, -tech-detect                      display technology in use based on wappalyzer dataset

arguments.description: path to a custom fingerprint file for technology detection
arguments.raw: -cff, -custom-fingerprint-file string  path to a custom fingerprint file for technology detection

arguments.description: display CPE (Common Platform Enumeration) based on awesome-search-queries
arguments.raw: -cpe                                   display CPE (Common Platform Enumeration) based on awesome-search-queries

arguments.description: display WordPress plugins and themes
arguments.raw: -wp, -wordpress                        display WordPress plugins and themes

arguments.description: display http request method
arguments.raw: -method                                display http request method

arguments.description: display server using websocket
arguments.raw: -ws, -websocket                        display server using websocket

arguments.description: display host ip
arguments.raw: -ip                                    display host ip

arguments.description: display host cname
arguments.raw: -cname                                 display host cname

arguments.description: get domain and subdomains from response body and header in jsonl/csv output
arguments.raw: -extract-fqdn, -efqdn                  get domain and subdomains from response body and header in jsonl/csv output

arguments.description: display host asn information
arguments.raw: -asn                                   display host asn information

arguments.description: display cdn/waf in use (default true)
arguments.raw: -cdn                                   display cdn/waf in use (default true)

arguments.description: display probe status
arguments.raw: -probe                                 display probe status

arguments.description: enable saving screenshot of the page using headless browser
arguments.raw: -ss, -screenshot                 enable saving screenshot of the page using headless browser

arguments.description: enable using local installed chrome for screenshot
arguments.raw: -system-chrome                   enable using local installed chrome for screenshot

arguments.description: start headless chrome with additional options
arguments.raw: -ho, -headless-options string[]  start headless chrome with additional options

arguments.description: enable excluding screenshot bytes from json output
arguments.raw: -esb, -exclude-screenshot-bytes  enable excluding screenshot bytes from json output

arguments.description: enable excluding headless header from json output
arguments.raw: -ehb, -exclude-headless-body     enable excluding headless header from json output

arguments.description: disable saving full page screenshot
arguments.raw: -no-screenshot-full-page         disable saving full page screenshot

arguments.description: set timeout for screenshot in seconds (default 10s)
arguments.raw: -st, -screenshot-timeout value   set timeout for screenshot in seconds (default 10s)

arguments.description: set idle time before taking screenshot in seconds (default 1s)
arguments.raw: -sid, -screenshot-idle value     set idle time before taking screenshot in seconds (default 1s)

arguments.description: execute JavaScript code after navigation
arguments.raw: -jsc, -javascript-code string[]  execute JavaScript code after navigation

arguments.description: match response with specified status code (-mc 200,302)
arguments.raw: -mc, -match-code string            match response with specified status code (-mc 200,302)

arguments.description: match response with specified content length (-ml 100,102)
arguments.raw: -ml, -match-length string          match response with specified content length (-ml 100,102)

arguments.description: match response body with specified line count (-mlc 423,532)
arguments.raw: -mlc, -match-line-count string     match response body with specified line count (-mlc 423,532)

arguments.description: match response body with specified word count (-mwc 43,55)
arguments.raw: -mwc, -match-word-count string     match response body with specified word count (-mwc 43,55)

arguments.description: match response with specified favicon hash (-mfc 1494302000)
arguments.raw: -mfc, -match-favicon string[]      match response with specified favicon hash (-mfc 1494302000)

arguments.description: match response with specified string (-ms admin)
arguments.raw: -ms, -match-string string[]        match response with specified string (-ms admin)

arguments.description: match response with specified regex (-mr admin)
arguments.raw: -mr, -match-regex string[]         match response with specified regex (-mr admin)

arguments.description: match host with specified cdn provider (cafe24, gocache, 云盾, 百度云加速, gcore, google, lgtelecom, skbroadband, 腾讯云, arvancloud, cdnetworks, kinx, qrator, 加速乐, cloudfront, fastly)
arguments.raw: -mcdn, -match-cdn string[]         match host with specified cdn provider (cafe24, gocache, 云盾, 百度云加速, gcore, google, lgtelecom, skbroadband, 腾讯云, arvancloud, cdnetworks, kinx, qrator, 加速乐, cloudfront, fastly)

arguments.description: match response with specified response time in seconds (-mrt '< 1')
arguments.raw: -mrt, -match-response-time string  match response with specified response time in seconds (-mrt '< 1')

arguments.description: match response with dsl expression condition
arguments.raw: -mdc, -match-condition string      match response with dsl expression condition

arguments.description: display response content with matched regex
arguments.raw: -er, -extract-regex string[]   display response content with matched regex

arguments.description: display response content matched by a pre-defined regex (url,ipv4,mail)
arguments.raw: -ep, -extract-preset string[]  display response content matched by a pre-defined regex (url,ipv4,mail)

arguments.description: filter response with specified status code (-fc 403,401)
arguments.raw: -fc, -filter-code string               filter response with specified status code (-fc 403,401)

arguments.description: filter response with specified page type (e.g. -fpt login,captcha,parked)
arguments.raw: -fpt, -filter-page-type string[]       filter response with specified page type (e.g. -fpt login,captcha,parked)

arguments.description: [DEPRECATED: use -fpt] filter response with ML based error page detection
arguments.raw: -fep, -filter-error-page               [DEPRECATED: use -fpt] filter response with ML based error page detection

arguments.description: filter out near-duplicate responses (only first response is retained)
arguments.raw: -fd, -filter-duplicates                filter out near-duplicate responses (only first response is retained)

arguments.description: filter response with specified content length (-fl 23,33)
arguments.raw: -fl, -filter-length string             filter response with specified content length (-fl 23,33)

arguments.description: filter response body with specified line count (-flc 423,532)
arguments.raw: -flc, -filter-line-count string        filter response body with specified line count (-flc 423,532)

arguments.description: filter response body with specified word count (-fwc 423,532)
arguments.raw: -fwc, -filter-word-count string        filter response body with specified word count (-fwc 423,532)

arguments.description: filter response with specified favicon hash (-ffc 1494302000)
arguments.raw: -ffc, -filter-favicon string[]         filter response with specified favicon hash (-ffc 1494302000)

arguments.description: filter response with specified string (-fs admin)
arguments.raw: -fs, -filter-string string[]           filter response with specified string (-fs admin)

arguments.description: filter response with specified regex (-fe admin)
arguments.raw: -fe, -filter-regex string[]            filter response with specified regex (-fe admin)

arguments.description: filter host with specified cdn provider (cafe24, gocache, 云盾, 百度云加速, gcore, google, lgtelecom, skbroadband, 腾讯云, arvancloud, cdnetworks, kinx, qrator, 加速乐, cloudfront, fastly)
arguments.raw: -fcdn, -filter-cdn string[]            filter host with specified cdn provider (cafe24, gocache, 云盾, 百度云加速, gcore, google, lgtelecom, skbroadband, 腾讯云, arvancloud, cdnetworks, kinx, qrator, 加速乐, cloudfront, fastly)

arguments.description: filter response with specified response time in seconds (-frt '> 1')
arguments.raw: -frt, -filter-response-time string     filter response with specified response time in seconds (-frt '> 1')

arguments.description: filter response with dsl expression condition
arguments.raw: -fdc, -filter-condition string         filter response with dsl expression condition

arguments.description: strips all tags in response. supported formats: html,xml (default html)
arguments.raw: -strip                                 strips all tags in response. supported formats: html,xml (default html)

arguments.description: list of fields to output (comma separated)
arguments.raw: -lof, -list-output-fields              list of fields to output (comma separated)

arguments.description: exclude output fields output based on a condition
arguments.raw: -eof, -exclude-output-fields string[]  exclude output fields output based on a condition

arguments.description: number of threads to use (default 50)
arguments.raw: -t, -threads int              number of threads to use (default 50)

arguments.description: maximum requests to send per second (default 150)
arguments.raw: -rl, -rate-limit int          maximum requests to send per second (default 150)

arguments.description: maximum number of requests to send per minute
arguments.raw: -rlm, -rate-limit-minute int  maximum number of requests to send per minute

arguments.description: probe all the ips associated with same host
arguments.raw: -pa, -probe-all-ips        probe all the ips associated with same host

arguments.description: ports to probe (nmap syntax: eg http:1,2-10,11,https:80)
arguments.raw: -p, -ports string[]        ports to probe (nmap syntax: eg http:1,2-10,11,https:80)

arguments.description: path or list of paths to probe (comma-separated, file)
arguments.raw: -path string               path or list of paths to probe (comma-separated, file)

arguments.description: send http probes on the extracted TLS domains (dns_name)
arguments.raw: -tls-probe                 send http probes on the extracted TLS domains (dns_name)

arguments.description: send http probes on the extracted CSP domains
arguments.raw: -csp-probe                 send http probes on the extracted CSP domains

arguments.description: perform TLS(SSL) data grabbing
arguments.raw: -tls-grab                  perform TLS(SSL) data grabbing

arguments.description: probe and display server supporting HTTP1.1 pipeline
arguments.raw: -pipeline                  probe and display server supporting HTTP1.1 pipeline

arguments.description: probe and display server supporting HTTP2
arguments.raw: -http2                     probe and display server supporting HTTP2

arguments.description: probe and display server supporting VHOST
arguments.raw: -vhost                     probe and display server supporting VHOST

arguments.description: list json output field keys name that support dsl matcher/filter
arguments.raw: -ldv, -list-dsl-variables  list json output field keys name that support dsl matcher/filter

arguments.description: update httpx to latest version
arguments.raw: -up, -update                 update httpx to latest version

arguments.description: disable automatic httpx update check
arguments.raw: -duc, -disable-update-check  disable automatic httpx update check

arguments.description: file to write output results
arguments.raw: -o, -output string                     file to write output results

arguments.description: filename to write output results in all formats
arguments.raw: -oa, -output-all                       filename to write output results in all formats

arguments.description: store http response to output directory
arguments.raw: -sr, -store-response                   store http response to output directory

arguments.description: store http response to custom directory
arguments.raw: -srd, -store-response-dir string       store http response to custom directory

arguments.description: omit response body in output
arguments.raw: -ob, -omit-body                        omit response body in output

arguments.description: store output in csv format
arguments.raw: -csv                                   store output in csv format

arguments.description: define output encoding
arguments.raw: -csvo, -csv-output-encoding string     define output encoding

arguments.description: store output in JSONL(ines) format
arguments.raw: -j, -json                              store output in JSONL(ines) format

arguments.description: store output in Markdown table format
arguments.raw: -md, -markdown                         store output in Markdown table format

arguments.description: include http response (headers) in JSON output (-json only)
arguments.raw: -irh, -include-response-header         include http response (headers) in JSON output (-json only)

arguments.description: include http request/response (headers + body) in JSON output (-json only)
arguments.raw: -irr, -include-response                include http request/response (headers + body) in JSON output (-json only)

arguments.description: include base64 encoded http request/response in JSON output (-json only)
arguments.raw: -irrb, -include-response-base64        include base64 encoded http request/response in JSON output (-json only)

arguments.description: include redirect http chain in JSON output (-json only)
arguments.raw: -include-chain                         include redirect http chain in JSON output (-json only)

arguments.description: include http redirect chain in responses (-sr only)
arguments.raw: -store-chain                           include http redirect chain in responses (-sr only)

arguments.description: include visual recon clusters (-ss and -sr only)
arguments.raw: -svrc, -store-vision-recon-cluster     include visual recon clusters (-ss and -sr only)

arguments.description: protocol to use (unknown, http11, http2 [experimental], http3 [experimental])
arguments.raw: -pr, -protocol string                  protocol to use (unknown, http11, http2 [experimental], http3 [experimental])

arguments.description: path to store filtered error pages (default "filtered_error_page.json")
arguments.raw: -fepp, -filter-error-page-path string  path to store filtered error pages (default "filtered_error_page.json")

arguments.description: store results in database
arguments.raw: -rdb, -result-db                       store results in database

arguments.description: path to database config file
arguments.raw: -rdbc, -result-db-config string        path to database config file

arguments.description: database type (mongodb, postgres, mysql)
arguments.raw: -rdbt, -result-db-type string          database type (mongodb, postgres, mysql)

arguments.description: database connection string
arguments.raw: -rdbcs, -result-db-conn string         database connection string

arguments.description: database name (default "httpx")
arguments.raw: -rdbn, -result-db-name string          database name (default "httpx")

arguments.description: table/collection name (default "results")
arguments.raw: -rdbtb, -result-db-table string        table/collection name (default "results")

arguments.description: batch size for database inserts (default 100)
arguments.raw: -rdbbs, -result-db-batch-size int      batch size for database inserts (default 100)

arguments.description: omit raw request/response data from database
arguments.raw: -rdbor, -result-db-omit-raw            omit raw request/response data from database

arguments.description: path to the httpx configuration file (default $HOME/.config/httpx/config.yaml)
arguments.raw: -config string                   path to the httpx configuration file (default $HOME/.config/httpx/config.yaml)

arguments.description: list of custom resolver (file or comma separated)
arguments.raw: -r, -resolvers string[]          list of custom resolver (file or comma separated)

arguments.description: allowed list of IP/CIDR's to process (file or comma separated)
arguments.raw: -allow string[]                  allowed list of IP/CIDR's to process (file or comma separated)

arguments.description: denied list of IP/CIDR's to process (file or comma separated)
arguments.raw: -deny string[]                   denied list of IP/CIDR's to process (file or comma separated)

arguments.description: custom TLS SNI name
arguments.raw: -sni, -sni-name string           custom TLS SNI name

arguments.description: enable Random User-Agent to use (default true)
arguments.raw: -random-agent                    enable Random User-Agent to use (default true)

arguments.description: set the Referer header to the current URL
arguments.raw: -auto-referer                    set the Referer header to the current URL

arguments.description: custom http headers to send with request
arguments.raw: -H, -header string[]             custom http headers to send with request

arguments.description: proxy (http|socks) to use (eg http://127.0.0.1:8080)
arguments.raw: -http-proxy, -proxy string       proxy (http|socks) to use (eg http://127.0.0.1:8080)

arguments.description: send raw requests skipping golang normalization
arguments.raw: -unsafe                          send raw requests skipping golang normalization

arguments.description: resume scan using resume.cfg
arguments.raw: -resume                          resume scan using resume.cfg

arguments.description: follow http redirects
arguments.raw: -fr, -follow-redirects           follow http redirects

arguments.description: max number of redirects to follow per host (default 10)
arguments.raw: -maxr, -max-redirects int        max number of redirects to follow per host (default 10)

arguments.description: follow redirects on the same host
arguments.raw: -fhr, -follow-host-redirects     follow redirects on the same host

arguments.description: respect HSTS response headers for redirect requests
arguments.raw: -rhsts, -respect-hsts            respect HSTS response headers for redirect requests

arguments.description: get a list of vhosts as input
arguments.raw: -vhost-input                     get a list of vhosts as input

arguments.description: request methods to probe, use 'all' to probe all HTTP methods
arguments.raw: -x string                        request methods to probe, use 'all' to probe all HTTP methods

arguments.description: post body to include in http request
arguments.raw: -body string                     post body to include in http request

arguments.description: stream mode - start elaborating input targets without sorting
arguments.raw: -s, -stream                      stream mode - start elaborating input targets without sorting

arguments.description: disable dedupe input items (only used with stream mode)
arguments.raw: -sd, -skip-dedupe                disable dedupe input items (only used with stream mode)

arguments.description: leave default http/https ports in host header (eg. http://host:80 - https://host:443
arguments.raw: -ldp, -leave-default-ports       leave default http/https ports in host header (eg. http://host:80 - https://host:443

arguments.description: use ztls library with autofallback to standard one for tls13
arguments.raw: -ztls                            use ztls library with autofallback to standard one for tls13

arguments.description: avoid decoding body
arguments.raw: -no-decode                       avoid decoding body

arguments.description: enable experimental client hello (ja3) tls randomization
arguments.raw: -tlsi, -tls-impersonate          enable experimental client hello (ja3) tls randomization

arguments.description: Disable Stdin processing
arguments.raw: -no-stdin                        Disable Stdin processing

arguments.description: experimental http api endpoint
arguments.raw: -hae, -http-api-endpoint string  experimental http api endpoint

arguments.description: path to the secret file for authentication
arguments.raw: -sf, -secret-file string         path to the secret file for authentication

arguments.description: run diagnostic check up
arguments.raw: -health-check, -hc        run diagnostic check up

arguments.description: display request/response content in cli
arguments.raw: -debug                    display request/response content in cli

arguments.description: display request content in cli
arguments.raw: -debug-req                display request content in cli

arguments.description: display response content in cli
arguments.raw: -debug-resp               display response content in cli

arguments.description: display httpx version
arguments.raw: -version                  display httpx version

arguments.description: display scan statistic
arguments.raw: -stats                    display scan statistic

arguments.description: optional httpx memory profile dump file
arguments.raw: -profile-mem string       optional httpx memory profile dump file

arguments.description: silent mode
arguments.raw: -silent                   silent mode

arguments.description: verbose mode
arguments.raw: -v, -verbose              verbose mode

arguments.description: number of seconds to wait between showing a statistics update (default: 5)
arguments.raw: -si, -stats-interval int  number of seconds to wait between showing a statistics update (default: 5)

arguments.description: disable colors in cli output
arguments.raw: -nc, -no-color            disable colors in cli output

arguments.description: trace
arguments.raw: -tr, -trace               trace

arguments.description: display both probed protocol (HTTPS and HTTP)
arguments.raw: -nf, -no-fallback                  display both probed protocol (HTTPS and HTTP)

arguments.description: probe with protocol scheme specified in input
arguments.raw: -nfs, -no-fallback-scheme          probe with protocol scheme specified in input

arguments.description: max error count per host before skipping remaining path/s (default 30)
arguments.raw: -maxhr, -max-host-error int        max error count per host before skipping remaining path/s (default 30)

arguments.description: exclude host matching specified filter ('cdn', 'private-ips', cidr, ip, regex)
arguments.raw: -e, -exclude string[]              exclude host matching specified filter ('cdn', 'private-ips', cidr, ip, regex)

arguments.description: number of retries
arguments.raw: -retries int                       number of retries

arguments.description: timeout in seconds (default 10)
arguments.raw: -timeout int                       timeout in seconds (default 10)

arguments.description: duration between each http request (eg: 200ms, 1s) (default -1ns)
arguments.raw: -delay value                       duration between each http request (eg: 200ms, 1s) (default -1ns)

arguments.description: max response size to save in bytes (default 512000000)
arguments.raw: -rsts, -response-size-to-save int  max response size to save in bytes (default 512000000)

arguments.description: max response size to read in bytes (default 512000000)
arguments.raw: -rstr, -response-size-to-read int  max response size to read in bytes (default 512000000)

arguments.description: configure projectdiscovery cloud (pdcp) api key (default true)
arguments.raw: -auth                           configure projectdiscovery cloud (pdcp) api key (default true)

arguments.description: configure projectdiscovery cloud (pdcp) api key credential file
arguments.raw: -ac, -auth-config string        configure projectdiscovery cloud (pdcp) api key credential file

arguments.description: upload / view output in projectdiscovery cloud (pdcp) UI dashboard
arguments.raw: -pd, -dashboard                 upload / view output in projectdiscovery cloud (pdcp) UI dashboard

arguments.description: upload asset results to given team id (optional)
arguments.raw: -tid, -team-id string           upload asset results to given team id (optional)

arguments.description: upload new assets to existing asset id (optional)
arguments.raw: -aid, -asset-id string          upload new assets to existing asset id (optional)

arguments.description: assets group name to set (optional)
arguments.raw: -aname, -asset-name string      assets group name to set (optional)

arguments.description: upload httpx output file (jsonl) in projectdiscovery cloud (pdcp) UI dashboard
arguments.raw: -pdu, -dashboard-upload string  upload httpx output file (jsonl) in projectdiscovery cloud (pdcp) UI dashboard

commands.raw_help:
root@kali:~# httpx-toolkit -h
 httpx is a fast and multi-purpose HTTP toolkit that allows running multiple probes using the retryablehttp library.
 
 Usage:
 httpx-toolkit [flags]
 
 Flags:
 INPUT:
  -l, -list string         input file containing list of hosts to process
  -rr, -request string     file containing raw request
  -u, -target string[]     input target host(s) to probe
  -im, -input-mode string  mode of input file (burp)
 
 PROBES:
  -sc, -status-code                      display response status-code
  -cl, -content-length                   display response content-length
  -ct, -content-type                     display response content-type
  -location                              display response redirect location
  -favicon                               display mmh3 hash for '/favicon.ico' file
  -hash string                           display response body hash (supported: md5,mmh3,simhash,sha1,sha256,sha512)
  -jarm                                  display jarm fingerprint hash
  -rt, -response-time                    display response time
  -lc, -line-count                       display response body line count
  -wc, -word-count                       display response body word count
  -title                                 display page title
  -bp, -body-preview                     display first N characters of response body (default 100)
  -server, -web-server                   display server name
  -td, -tech-detect                      display technology in use based on wappalyzer dataset
  -cff, -custom-fingerprint-file string  path to a custom fingerprint file for technology detection
  -cpe                                   display CPE (Common Platform Enumeration) based on awesome-search-queries
  -wp, -wordpress                        display WordPress plugins and themes
  -method                                display http request method
  -ws, -websocket                        display server using websocket
  -ip                                    display host ip
  -cname                                 display host cname
  -extract-fqdn, -efqdn                  get domain and subdomains from response body and header in jsonl/csv output
  -asn                                   display host asn information
  -cdn                                   display cdn/waf in use (default true)
  -probe                                 display probe status
 
 HEADLESS:
  -ss, -screenshot                 enable saving screenshot of the page using headless browser
  -system-chrome                   enable using local installed chrome for screenshot
  -ho, -headless-options string[]  start headless chrome with additional options
  -esb, -exclude-screenshot-bytes  enable excluding screenshot bytes from json output
  -ehb, -exclude-headless-body     enable excluding headless header from json output
  -no-screenshot-full-page         disable saving full page screenshot
  -st, -screenshot-timeout value   set timeout for screenshot in seconds (default 10s)
  -sid, -screenshot-idle value     set idle time before taking screenshot in seconds (default 1s)
  -jsc, -javascript-code string[]  execute JavaScript code after navigation
 
 MATCHERS:
  -mc, -match-code string            match response with specified status code (-mc 200,302)
  -ml, -match-length string          match response with specified content length (-ml 100,102)
  -mlc, -match-line-count string     match response body with specified line count (-mlc 423,532)
  -mwc, -match-word-count string     match response body with specified word count (-mwc 43,55)
  -mfc, -match-favicon string[]      match response with specified favicon hash (-mfc 1494302000)
  -ms, -match-string string[]        match response with specified string (-ms admin)
  -mr, -match-regex string[]         match response with specified regex (-mr admin)
  -mcdn, -match-cdn string[]         match host with specified cdn provider (cafe24, gocache, 云盾, 百度云加速, gcore, google, lgtelecom, skbroadband, 腾讯云, arvancloud, cdnetworks, kinx, qrator, 加速乐, cloudfront, fastly)
  -mrt, -match-response-time string  match response with specified response time in seconds (-mrt '< 1')
  -mdc, -match-condition string      match response with dsl expression condition
 
 EXTRACTOR:
  -er, -extract-regex string[]   display response content with matched regex
  -ep, -extract-preset string[]  display response content matched by a pre-defined regex (url,ipv4,mail)
 
 FILTERS:
  -fc, -filter-code string               filter response with specified status code (-fc 403,401)
  -fpt, -filter-page-type string[]       filter response with specified page type (e.g. -fpt login,captcha,parked)
  -fep, -filter-error-page               [DEPRECATED: use -fpt] filter response with ML based error page detection
  -fd, -filter-duplicates                filter out near-duplicate responses (only first response is retained)
  -fl, -filter-length string             filter response with specified content length (-fl 23,33)
  -flc, -filter-line-count string        filter response body with specified line count (-flc 423,532)
  -fwc, -filter-word-count string        filter response body with specified word count (-fwc 423,532)
  -ffc, -filter-favicon string[]         filter response with specified favicon hash (-ffc 1494302000)
  -fs, -filter-string string[]           filter response with specified string (-fs admin)
  -fe, -filter-regex string[]            filter response with specified regex (-fe admin)
  -fcdn, -filter-cdn string[]            filter host with specified cdn provider (cafe24, gocache, 云盾, 百度云加速, gcore, google, lgtelecom, skbroadband, 腾讯云, arvancloud, cdnetworks, kinx, qrator, 加速乐, cloudfront, fastly)
  -frt, -filter-response-time string     filter response with specified response time in seconds (-frt '> 1')
  -fdc, -filter-condition string         filter response with dsl expression condition
  -strip                                 strips all tags in response. supported formats: html,xml (default html)
  -lof, -list-output-fields              list of fields to output (comma separated)
  -eof, -exclude-output-fields string[]  exclude output fields output based on a condition
 
 RATE-LIMIT:
  -t, -threads int              number of threads to use (default 50)
  -rl, -rate-limit int          maximum requests to send per second (default 150)
  -rlm, -rate-limit-minute int  maximum number of requests to send per minute
 
 MISCELLANEOUS:
  -pa, -probe-all-ips        probe all the ips associated with same host
  -p, -ports string[]        ports to probe (nmap syntax: eg http:1,2-10,11,https:80)
  -path string               path or list of paths to probe (comma-separated, file)
  -tls-probe                 send http probes on the extracted TLS domains (dns_name)
  -csp-probe                 send http probes on the extracted CSP domains
  -tls-grab                  perform TLS(SSL) data grabbing
  -pipeline                  probe and display server supporting HTTP1.1 pipeline
  -http2                     probe and display server supporting HTTP2
  -vhost                     probe and display server supporting VHOST
  -ldv, -list-dsl-variables  list json output field keys name that support dsl matcher/filter
 
 UPDATE:
  -up, -update                 update httpx to latest version
  -duc, -disable-update-check  disable automatic httpx update check
 
 OUTPUT:
  -o, -output string                     file to write output results
  -oa, -output-all                       filename to write output results in all formats
  -sr, -store-response                   store http response to output directory
  -srd, -store-response-dir string       store http response to custom directory
  -ob, -omit-body                        omit response body in output
  -csv                                   store output in csv format
  -csvo, -csv-output-encoding string     define output encoding
  -j, -json                              store output in JSONL(ines) format
  -md, -markdown                         store output in Markdown table format
  -irh, -include-response-header         include http response (headers) in JSON output (-json only)
  -irr, -include-response                include http request/response (headers + body) in JSON output (-json only)
  -irrb, -include-response-base64        include base64 encoded http request/response in JSON output (-json only)
  -include-chain                         include redirect http chain in JSON output (-json only)
  -store-chain                           include http redirect chain in responses (-sr only)
  -svrc, -store-vision-recon-cluster     include visual recon clusters (-ss and -sr only)
  -pr, -protocol string                  protocol to use (unknown, http11, http2 [experimental], http3 [experimental])
  -fepp, -filter-error-page-path string  path to store filtered error pages (default "filtered_error_page.json")
  -rdb, -result-db                       store results in database
  -rdbc, -result-db-config string        path to database config file
  -rdbt, -result-db-type string          database type (mongodb, postgres, mysql)
  -rdbcs, -result-db-conn string         database connection string
  -rdbn, -result-db-name string          database name (default "httpx")
  -rdbtb, -result-db-table string        table/collection name (default "results")
  -rdbbs, -result-db-batch-size int      batch size for database inserts (default 100)
  -rdbor, -result-db-omit-raw            omit raw request/response data from database
 
 CONFIGURATIONS:
  -config string                   path to the httpx configuration file (default $HOME/.config/httpx/config.yaml)
  -r, -resolvers string[]          list of custom resolver (file or comma separated)
  -allow string[]                  allowed list of IP/CIDR's to process (file or comma separated)
  -deny string[]                   denied list of IP/CIDR's to process (file or comma separated)
  -sni, -sni-name string           custom TLS SNI name
  -random-agent                    enable Random User-Agent to use (default true)
  -auto-referer                    set the Referer header to the current URL
  -H, -header string[]             custom http headers to send with request
  -http-proxy, -proxy string       proxy (http|socks) to use (eg http://127.0.0.1:8080)
  -unsafe                          send raw requests skipping golang normalization
  -resume                          resume scan using resume.cfg
  -fr, -follow-redirects           follow http redirects
  -maxr, -max-redirects int        max number of redirects to follow per host (default 10)
  -fhr, -follow-host-redirects     follow redirects on the same host
  -rhsts, -respect-hsts            respect HSTS response headers for redirect requests
  -vhost-input                     get a list of vhosts as input
  -x string                        request methods to probe, use 'all' to probe all HTTP methods
  -body string                     post body to include in http request
  -s, -stream                      stream mode - start elaborating input targets without sorting
  -sd, -skip-dedupe                disable dedupe input items (only used with stream mode)
  -ldp, -leave-default-ports       leave default http/https ports in host header (eg. http://host:80 - https://host:443
  -ztls                            use ztls library with autofallback to standard one for tls13
  -no-decode                       avoid decoding body
  -tlsi, -tls-impersonate          enable experimental client hello (ja3) tls randomization
  -no-stdin                        Disable Stdin processing
  -hae, -http-api-endpoint string  experimental http api endpoint
  -sf, -secret-file string         path to the secret file for authentication
 
 DEBUG:
  -health-check, -hc        run diagnostic check up
  -debug                    display request/response content in cli
  -debug-req                display request content in cli
  -debug-resp               display response content in cli
  -version                  display httpx version
  -stats                    display scan statistic
  -profile-mem string       optional httpx memory profile dump file
  -silent                   silent mode
  -v, -verbose              verbose mode
  -si, -stats-interval int  number of seconds to wait between showing a statistics update (default: 5)
  -nc, -no-color            disable colors in cli output
  -tr, -trace               trace
 
 OPTIMIZATIONS:
  -nf, -no-fallback                  display both probed protocol (HTTPS and HTTP)
  -nfs, -no-fallback-scheme          probe with protocol scheme specified in input 
  -maxhr, -max-host-error int        max error count per host before skipping remaining path/s (default 30)
  -e, -exclude string[]              exclude host matching specified filter ('cdn', 'private-ips', cidr, ip, regex)
  -retries int                       number of retries
  -timeout int                       timeout in seconds (default 10)
  -delay value                       duration between each http request (eg: 200ms, 1s) (default -1ns)
  -rsts, -response-size-to-save int  max response size to save in bytes (default 512000000)
  -rstr, -response-size-to-read int  max response size to read in bytes (default 512000000)
 
 CLOUD:
  -auth                           configure projectdiscovery cloud (pdcp) api key (default true)
  -ac, -auth-config string        configure projectdiscovery cloud (pdcp) api key credential file
  -pd, -dashboard                 upload / view output in projectdiscovery cloud (pdcp) UI dashboard
  -tid, -team-id string           upload asset results to given team id (optional)
  -aid, -asset-id string          upload new assets to existing asset id (optional)
  -aname, -asset-name string      assets group name to set (optional)
  -pdu, -dashboard-upload string  upload httpx output file (jsonl) in projectdiscovery cloud (pdcp) UI dashboard

local_help.--help:
httpx is a fast and multi-purpose HTTP toolkit that allows running multiple probes using the retryablehttp library.

Usage:
  httpx-toolkit [flags]

Flags:
INPUT:
   -l, -list string         input file containing list of hosts to process
   -rr, -request string     file containing raw request
   -u, -target string[]     input target host(s) to probe
   -im, -input-mode string  mode of input file (burp)

PROBES:
   -sc, -status-code                      display response status-code
   -cl, -content-length                   display response content-length
   -ct, -content-type                     display response content-type
   -location                              display response redirect location
   -favicon                               display mmh3 hash for '/favicon.ico' file
   -hash string                           display response body hash (supported: md5,mmh3,simhash,sha1,sha256,sha512)
   -jarm                                  display jarm fingerprint hash
   -rt, -response-time                    display response time
   -lc, -line-count                       display response body line count
   -wc, -word-count                       display response body word count
   -title                                 display page title
   -bp, -body-preview                     display first N characters of response body (default 100)
   -server, -web-server                   display server name
   -td, -tech-detect                      display technology in use based on wappalyzer dataset
   -cff, -custom-fingerprint-file string  path to a custom fingerprint file for technology detection
   -cpe                                   display CPE (Common Platform Enumeration) based on awesome-search-queries
   -wp, -wordpress                        display WordPress plugins and themes
   -method                                display http request method
   -ws, -websocket                        display server using websocket
   -ip                                    display host ip
   -cname                                 display host cname
   -extract-fqdn, -efqdn                  get domain and subdomains from response body and header in jsonl/csv output
   -asn                                   display host asn information
   -cdn                                   display cdn/waf in use (default true)
   -probe                                 display probe status

HEADLESS:
   -ss, -screenshot                 enable saving screenshot of the page using headless browser
   -system-chrome                   enable using local installed chrome for screenshot
   -ho, -headless-options string[]  start headless chrome with additional options
   -esb, -exclude-screenshot-bytes  enable excluding screenshot bytes from json output
   -ehb, -exclude-headless-body     enable excluding headless header from json output
   -no-screenshot-full-page         disable saving full page screenshot
   -st, -screenshot-timeout value   set timeout for screenshot in seconds (default 10s)
   -sid, -screenshot-idle value     set idle time before taking screenshot in seconds (default 1s)
   -jsc, -javascript-code string[]  execute JavaScript code after navigation

MATCHERS:
   -mc, -match-code string            match response with specified status code (-mc 200,302)
   -ml, -match-length string          match response with specified content length (-ml 100,102)
   -mlc, -match-line-count string     match response body with specified line count (-mlc 423,532)
   -mwc, -match-word-count string     match response body with specified word count (-mwc 43,55)
   -mfc, -match-favicon string[]      match response with specified favicon hash (-mfc 1494302000)
   -ms, -match-string string[]        match response with specified string (-ms admin)
   -mr, -match-regex string[]         match response with specified regex (-mr admin)
   -mcdn, -match-cdn string[]         match host with specified cdn provider (加速乐, cafe24, cdnetworks, gcore, skbroadband, 云盾, fastly, google, kinx, lgtelecom, qrator, 百度云加速, 腾讯云, arvancloud, cloudfront, gocache)
   -mrt, -match-response-time string  match response with specified response time in seconds (-mrt '< 1')
   -mdc, -match-condition string      match response with dsl expression condition

EXTRACTOR:
   -er, -extract-regex string[]   display response content with matched regex
   -ep, -extract-preset string[]  display response content matched by a pre-defined regex (url,ipv4,mail)

FILTERS:
   -fc, -filter-code string               filter response with specified status code (-fc 403,401)
   -fpt, -filter-page-type string[]       filter response with specified page type (e.g. -fpt login,captcha,parked)
   -fep, -filter-error-page               [DEPRECATED: use -fpt] filter response with ML based error page detection
   -fd, -filter-duplicates                filter out near-duplicate responses (only first response is retained)
   -fl, -filter-length string             filter response with specified content length (-fl 23,33)
   -flc, -filter-line-count string        filter response body with specified line count (-flc 423,532)
   -fwc, -filter-word-count string        filter response body with specified word count (-fwc 423,532)
   -ffc, -filter-favicon string[]         filter response with specified favicon hash (-ffc 1494302000)
   -fs, -filter-string string[]           filter response with specified string (-fs admin)
   -fe, -filter-regex string[]            filter response with specified regex (-fe admin)
   -fcdn, -filter-cdn string[]            filter host with specified cdn provider (加速乐, cafe24, cdnetworks, gcore, skbroadband, 云盾, fastly, google, kinx, lgtelecom, qrator, 百度云加速, 腾讯云, arvancloud, cloudfront, gocache)
   -frt, -filter-response-time string     filter response with specified response time in seconds (-frt '> 1')
   -fdc, -filter-condition string         filter response with dsl expression condition
   -strip                                 strips all tags in response. supported formats: html,xml (default html)
   -lof, -list-output-fields              list of fields to output (comma separated)
   -eof, -exclude-output-fields string[]  exclude output fields output based on a condition

RATE-LIMIT:
   -t, -threads int              number of threads to use (default 50)
   -rl, -rate-limit int          maximum requests to send per second (default 150)
   -rlm, -rate-limit-minute int  maximum number of requests to send per minute

MISCELLANEOUS:
   -pa, -probe-all-ips        probe all the ips associated with same host
   -p, -ports string[]        ports to probe (nmap syntax: eg http:1,2-10,11,https:80)
   -path string               path or list of paths to probe (comma-separated, file)
   -tls-probe                 send http probes on the extracted TLS domains (dns_name)
   -csp-probe                 send http probes on the extracted CSP domains
   -tls-grab                  perform TLS(SSL) data grabbing
   -pipeline                  probe and display server supporting HTTP1.1 pipeline
   -http2                     probe and display server supporting HTTP2
   -vhost                     probe and display server supporting VHOST
   -ldv, -list-dsl-variables  list json output field keys name that support dsl matcher/filter

UPDATE:
   -up, -update                 update httpx to latest version
   -duc, -disable-update-check  disable automatic httpx update check

OUTPUT:
   -o, -output string                     file to write output results
   -oa, -output-all                       filename to write output results in all formats
   -sr, -store-response                   store http response to output directory
   -srd, -store-response-dir string       store http response to custom directory
   -ob, -omit-body                        omit response body in output
   -csv                                   store output in csv format
   -csvo, -csv-output-encoding string     define output encoding
   -j, -json                              store output in JSONL(ines) format
   -md, -markdown                         store output in Markdown table format
   -irh, -include-response-header         include http response (headers) in JSON output (-json only)
   -irr, -include-response                include http request/response (headers + body) in JSON output (-json only)
   -irrb, -include-response-base64        include base64 encoded http request/response in JSON output (-json only)
   -include-chain                         include redirect http chain in JSON output (-json only)
   -store-chain                           include http redirect chain in responses (-sr only)
   -svrc, -store-vision-recon-cluster     include visual recon clusters (-ss and -sr only)
   -pr, -protocol string                  protocol to use (unknown, http11, http2 [experimental], http3 [experimental])
   -fepp, -filter-error-page-path string  path to store filtered error pages (default "filtered_error_page.json")
   -rdb, -result-db                       store results in database
   -rdbc, -result-db-config string        path to database config file
   -rdbt, -result-db-type string          database type (mongodb, postgres, mysql)
   -rdbcs, -result-db-conn string         database connection string
   -rdbn, -result-db-name string          database name (default "httpx")
   -rdbtb, -result-db-table string        table/collection name (default "results")
   -rdbbs, -result-db-batch-size int      batch size for database inserts (default 100)
   -rdbor, -result-db-omit-raw            omit raw request/response data from database

CONFIGURATIONS:
   -config string                   path to the httpx configuration file (default $HOME/.config/httpx/config.yaml)
   -r, -resolvers string[]          list of custom resolver (file or comma separated)
   -allow string[]                  allowed list of IP/CIDR's to process (file or comma separated)
   -deny string[]                   denied list of IP/CIDR's to process (file or comma separated)
   -sni, -sni-name string           custom TLS SNI name
   -random-agent                    enable Random User-Agent to use (default true)
   -auto-referer                    set the Referer header to the current URL
   -H, -header string[]             custom http headers to send with request
   -http-proxy, -proxy string       proxy (http|socks) to use (eg http://127.0.0.1:8080)
   -unsafe                          send raw requests skipping golang normalization
   -resume                          resume scan using resume.cfg
   -fr, -follow-redirects           follow http redirects
   -maxr, -max-redirects int        max number of redirects to follow per host (default 10)
   -fhr, -follow-host-redirects     follow redirects on the same host
   -rhsts, -respect-hsts            respect HSTS response headers for redirect requests
   -vhost-input                     get a list of vhosts as input
   -x string                        request methods to probe, use 'all' to probe all HTTP methods
   -body string                     post body to include in http request
   -s, -stream                      stream mode - start elaborating input targets without sorting
   -sd, -skip-dedupe                disable dedupe input items (only used with stream mode)
   -ldp, -leave-default-ports       leave default http/https ports in host header (eg. http://host:80 - https://host:443
   -ztls                            use ztls library with autofallback to standard one for tls13
   -no-decode                       avoid decoding body
   -tlsi, -tls-impersonate          enable experimental client hello (ja3) tls randomization
   -no-stdin                        Disable Stdin processing
   -hae, -http-api-endpoint string  experimental http api endpoint
   -sf, -secret-file string         path to the secret file for authentication

DEBUG:
   -health-check, -hc        run diagnostic check up
   -debug                    display request/response content in cli

   -debug-resp               display response content in cli
   -version                  display httpx version
   -stats                    display scan statistic
   -profile-mem string       optional httpx memory profile dump file

   -v, -verbose              verbose mode
   -si, -stats-interval int  number of seconds to wait between showing a statistics update (default: 5)
   -nc, -no-color            disable colors in cli output
   -tr, -trace               trace

OPTIMIZATIONS:
   -nf, -no-fallback                  display both probed protocol (HTTPS and HTTP)
   -nfs, -no-fallback-scheme          probe with protocol scheme specified in input 
   -maxhr, -max-host-error int        max error count per host before skipping remaining path/s (default 30)

   -retries int                       number of retries

   -delay value                       duration between each http request (eg: 200ms, 1s) (default -1ns)
   -rsts, -response-size-to-save int  max response size to save in bytes (default 512000000)
   -rstr, -response-size-to-read int  max response size to read in bytes (default 512000000)

CLOUD:
   -auth                           configure projectdiscovery cloud (pdcp) api key (default true)
   -ac, -auth-config string        configure projectdiscovery cloud (pdcp) api key credential file

   -duc, -disable-update-check  disable automatic httpx update check

OUTPUT:
   -o, -output string                     file to write output results

   -irr, -include-response                include http request/response (headers + body) in JSON output (-json only)
   -irrb, -include-response-base64        include base64 encoded http request/response in JSON output (-json only)
   -include-chain                         include redirect http chain in JSON output (-json only)
   -store-chain                           include http redirect chain in responses (-sr only)
   -svrc, -store-vision-recon-cluster     include visual recon clusters (-ss and -sr only)
   -pr, -protocol string                  protocol to use (unknown, http11, http2 [experimental], http3 [experimental])
   -fepp, -filter-error-page-path string  path to store filtered error pages (default "filtered_error_page.json")
   -rdb, -result-db                       store results in database
   -rdbc, -result-db-config string        path to database config file
   -rdbt, -result-db-type string          database type (mongodb, postgres, mysql)
   -rdbcs, -result-db-conn string         database connection string
   -rdbn, -result-db-name string          database name (default "httpx")
   -rdbtb, -result-db-table string        table/collection name (default "results")
   -rdbbs, -result-db-batch-size int      batch size for database inserts (default 100)
   -rdbor, -result-db-omit-raw            omit raw request/response data from database

CONFIGURATIONS:
   -config string                   path to the httpx configuration file (default $HOME/.config/httpx/config.yaml)
   -r, -resolvers string[]          list of custom resolver (file or comma separated)
   -allow string[]                  allowed list of IP/CIDR's to process (file or comma separated)
   -deny string[]                   denied list of IP/CIDR's to process (file or comma separated)
   -sni, -sni-name string           custom TLS SNI name
   -random-agent                    enable Random User-Agent to use (default true)
   -auto-referer                    set the Referer header to the current URL
   -H, -header string[]             custom http headers to send with request
   -http-proxy, -proxy string       proxy (http|socks) to use (eg http://127.0.0.1:8080)
   -unsafe                          send raw requests skipping golang normalization
   -resume                          resume scan using resume.cfg
   -fr, -follow-redirects           follow http redirects
   -maxr, -max-redirects int        max number of redirects to follow per host (default 10)
   -fhr, -follow-host-redirects     follow redirects on the same host
   -rhsts, -respect-hsts            respect HSTS response headers for redirect requests
   -vhost-input                     get a list of vhosts as input
   -x string                        request methods to probe, use 'all' to probe all HTTP methods
   -body string                     post body to include in http request
   -s, -stream                      stream mode - start elaborating input targets without sorting
   -sd, -skip-dedupe                disable dedupe input items (only used with stream mode)
   -ldp, -leave-default-ports       leave default http/https ports in host header (eg. http://host:80 - https://host:443
   -ztls                            use ztls library with autofallback to standard one for tls13
   -no-decode                       avoid decoding body
   -tlsi, -tls-impersonate          enable experimental client hello (ja3) tls randomization
   -no-stdin                        Disable Stdin processing
   -hae, -http-api-endpoint string  experimental http api endpoint
   -sf, -secret-file string         path to the secret file for authentication

DEBUG:
   -health-check, -hc        run diagnostic check up
   -debug                    display request/response content in cli
   -debug-req                display request content in cli
   -debug-resp               display response content in cli
   -version                  display httpx version
   -stats                    display scan statistic
   -profile-mem string       optional httpx memory profile dump file
   -silent                   silent mode
   -v, -verbose              verbose mode
   -si, -stats-interval int  number of seconds to wait between showing a statistics update (default: 5)
   -nc, -no-color            disable colors in cli output
   -tr, -trace               trace

OPTIMIZATIONS:
   -nf, -no-fallback                  display both probed protocol (HTTPS and HTTP)
   -nfs, -no-fallback-scheme          probe with protocol scheme specified in input 
   -maxhr, -max-host-error int        max error count per host before skipping remaining path/s (default 30)
   -e, -exclude string[]              exclude host matching specified filter ('cdn', 'private-ips', cidr, ip, regex)
   -retries int                       number of retries
   -timeout int                       timeout in seconds (default 10)
   -delay value                       duration between each http request (eg: 200ms, 1s) (default -1ns)
   -rsts, -response-size-to-save int  max response size to save in bytes (default 512000000)
   -rstr, -response-size-to-read int  max response size to read in bytes (default 512000000)

CLOUD:
   -auth                           configure projectdiscovery cloud (pdcp) api key (default true)
   -ac, -auth-config string        configure projectdiscovery cloud (pdcp) api key credential file
   -pd, -dashboard                 upload / view output in projectdiscovery cloud (pdcp) UI dashboard
   -tid, -team-id string           upload asset results to given team id (optional)
   -aid, -asset-id string          upload new assets to existing asset id (optional)
   -aname, -asset-name string      assets group name to set (optional)
   -pdu, -dashboard-upload string  upload httpx output file (jsonl) in projectdiscovery cloud (pdcp) UI dashboard

apt_metadata.description:
fast and multi-purpose HTTP toolkit
This package contains the httpX toolkit developed by ProjectDiscovery. It's a
fast and multi-purpose HTTP toolkit allow to run multiple probers using
retryablehttp library, it is designed to maintain the result reliability with
increased threads.
.
Features
* Simple and modular code base making it easy to contribute.
* Fast And fully configurable flags to probe multiple elements.
* Supports multiple HTTP based probings.
* Smart auto fallback from https to http as default.
* Supports hosts, URLs and CIDR as input.
* Handles edge cases doing retries, backoffs etc for handling WAFs.
.
This tool is packaged as 'httpx-toolkit' to avoid confusion and conflicts
with the package python3-httpx that provides a script /usr/bin/httpx.

use_cases: Use as a candidate tool in the chain planner; review provenance before use.
Indexing only — no automated invocation. The operator must manually inspect the repository before adding to a toolchain.
